title: Reis moon ocean symbology
wikitext:
[]: Rei's moon/ocean symbology             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Mar 31, 2008 4:31 pm I've come to think that part of why Rei is on occasion given a moon/ocean correlation, other than Lilith originating with the moon or whatever celestial body caused the first impact, involves the moon and the ocean as two of the most primitive, ancient anthropological maternal symbols. It ties into the series' massive, overlying "mother" theme. what do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 31, 2008 5:05 pm There's an archival thread here : http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=4438&start=0 which may be relevant. Also http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=4062

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 31, 2008 6:16 pm Yeah, the water/Rei/motherhood symbolism is some of the more overt symbolism in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Mar 31, 2008 7:32 pm Though I am aware the presence of these two symbols and their relationship with Rei, I must admit to remaining entirely confounded as to their meaning and/or significance. What exactly are these images trying to tell us? Can anyone offer up their suggestions or interpretations?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 31, 2008 7:39 pm The Rei/Moon connection always confused me. Because Lilith isn't from the White Moon, but the Black Moon. But I never read that entire thread on that. But the part one Evapedia, where it talks about Rei's connection with the moon and Asuka's first appearance silhouetted by the sun being symbolism of their opposition made sense to me. Rei = Moon. Asuka = Sun. Rei =/= Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 31, 2008 7:57 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Though I am aware the presence of these two symbols and their relationship with Rei, I must admit to remaining entirely confounded as to their meaning and/or significance. What exactly are these images trying to tell us? Can anyone offer up their suggestions or interpretations?

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Mon Mar 31, 2008 9:18 pm Sachi_13 wrote: Rei = Moon. Asuka = Sun. Rei =/= Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Tue Apr 01, 2008 3:10 am I think the emphasis that the other topic lacked is on Lilith as the mother to all life we know. That's the barebones explanation. Stretching it, you can see our interpreting the ocean or moon as motherly because life certainly originated in the ocean, and the moon brought Lilith to earth in a collision (but even beyond that, the moon's been a maternal deity since prehistory). I love anthropology, so this seems to come together perfectly to me. By extension, I think the glimpses of blood spattered across the moon in EoE could be a symbol of childbirth/rebirth.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 01, 2008 6:59 pm The Moon is connected to Rei because it was created during the First Impact when Lilith landed on proto-Earth, causing the Moon to splinter from the nascent planet. Asuka's Sun metaphor is also relevant because she thinks everything revolves around her, and she shines so brightly and boldly in the series that she's pretty much the most attention-grabbing character, outshining most others in the eyes of the fandom, save for the Moon, which shall always attract the philosophical and romantic interests of many. Damn, I'm good.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Apr 01, 2008 7:59 pm Apart from that, you might say that Shinji is dependent on Asuka emotionally- his feelings revolve around her as the Earth revolves around the Sun. Rei is dependent on Shinji emotionally- after all, she ditches Gendo for him at the end- her feelings revolve around him like the Moon revolves around the Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 01, 2008 11:39 pm Which thus makes Shinji the Earth, which is thematically appropriate since he's the foundation that the plot is pretty much built on as the protagonist.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Wed Apr 02, 2008 2:15 am Asuka's Sun metaphor is also relevant because she thinks everything revolves around her, and she shines so brightly and boldly in the series that she's pretty much the most attention-grabbing character, outshining most others in the eyes of the fandom, save for the Moon, which shall always attract the philosophical and romantic interests of many. I think that's reading way too far in. other than in EoE when she reaches up to the sun, she's seen with the sun once, in her introduction. Rei's moon metaphor is overt but sublime. it's hard to say Asuka had any intentional link to the sun at all, let alone a symbolic one past just a visual one.

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Wed Apr 02, 2008 2:42 am FallenTabris wrote: I think that's reading way too far in. other than in EoE when she reaches up to the sun, she's seen with the sun once, in her introduction. Rei's moon metaphor is overt but sublime. it's hard to say Asuka had any intentional link to the sun at all, let alone a symbolic one past just a visual one.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Apr 02, 2008 5:57 am majlund wrote: I can post screenies but that won't be until 36 hours from now...

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Wed Apr 02, 2008 6:16 am Good job Zuggy. Somehow I had a feeling you'd help me out here

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Wed Apr 02, 2008 1:36 pm ..her circle's the exact color of her hair. Same for Rei. Someone get a screencap of Misato's.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Apr 02, 2008 2:24 pm FallenTabris wrote: Someone get a screencap of Misato's.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Apr 03, 2008 3:27 pm I think you're off in a direction Anno would sooner lul than conceive of.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Apr 03, 2008 3:43 pm In terms of the five elements they come up overtly in Rei's ramble in episode #14, and whenever you're looking at the Seele committee from above you're seeing the five colours of the elements (white, red, yellow, green, blue) arranged in the Japanese pentagram's order (the same as the Western one yet the elements differ in order) which is further sitting in a purple octagon. It wouldn't surprise me if the spotlights are supposed to be alluding to them then.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Apr 03, 2008 7:37 pm white, red, yellow, green, blue Oh shit, basic colors! you realize this could just as likely--actually, more likely--indicate the Order of the Eastern Star (AKA the Freemasons): http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Image:OrderEasternStar_logo_from_saucer.jpg When different nationalities and characters somehow correspond to elements, I think you're reading into something too deep, headfirst. I used to do that sort of elemental configuration with my favorite anime characters when I was 15, and I don't mean that condescendingly at all, but rather, just because things seem to fit elementally to you doesn't mean other people are going to feel the same: Asuka's fiery, Rei's cold, Shinji's sitting on a chair with a world backdrop, and where are we now? We're trying too hard to look for things that may not be there. Here, let me blow all of our minds. Click on the star wiki link I showed above. Now check out Seele's colorings: http://evageeks.org/FGP/Image:Gendo_With_Committee.jpg holy shit, the colors correspond perfectly! NOWAI!!! seriously, they do. so are we dealing with Japanese shintoism or a freemason new world order? Or are we just trying too freakin hard? The coloring order may be intentional, but the "elements" in this case don't mean shit. yeah, I realize I'm being a dick.
